# obsidian-mcp-rs

> Rust-based MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for Obsidian vaults. Lets AI assistants (Claude, Cursor, etc.) read, write, search, and manage notes inside Obsidian vaults over stdio transport.

## What it does

- Exposes 15 MCP tools for full note lifecycle management: create, read, edit, delete, move, search, tag, frontmatter, links, daily notes
- Supports multiple vaults simultaneously via CLI arguments
- Ships as a single static binary ‚Äî no runtime required
- stdio by default; Streamable HTTP (`--http`) available via the optional `http` cargo feature
- Distributed as an npm package (`obsidian-mcp-rs`) with platform-specific binaries via optional dependencies

## Tools

- `read-note` ‚Äî read a note; `view: "outline"` returns just its headings, block refs and frontmatter keys. Capped at 400 lines by default; page on with `offset`/`limit`
- `create-note` ‚Äî create a new markdown note with content
- `edit-note` ‚Äî append, prepend, replace, or find-and-replace ‚Äî over the whole note, or scoped to one heading's section or one `^block-id`
- `delete-note` ‚Äî move a note to the vault's `.trash/` (recoverable); `permanent: true` erases it
- `move-note` ‚Äî move or rename a note, rewriting inbound `[[wikilinks]]` so they follow it. Omit `newFolder` to rename in place; pass `""` to move to the vault root
- `create-directory` ‚Äî create a directory inside a vault
- `search-vault` ‚Äî BM25-ranked search by content, filename, or tag (`tag:` prefix); paged via `limit`/`offset`. `regex: true` matches a pattern instead of words; `frontmatter: {"status": "active"}` keeps only notes carrying those fields (a list field matches when it contains the value), and works alone with an empty query
- `list-available-vaults` ‚Äî list all configured vaults
- `wikilinks` ‚Äî link graph: `backlinks`, `outgoing`, `broken`, `orphans`
- `frontmatter` ‚Äî read or write any YAML frontmatter key (`get`/`set`/`remove`), preserving the rest of the block
- `vault-info` ‚Äî what's in the vault: `tags` (with counts), `recent`, `stats`
- `periodic` ‚Äî daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly notes (`get`/`create`/`list`), using Obsidian's own name, folder and template settings
- `add-tags` ‚Äî add tags to frontmatter and/or content
- `remove-tags` ‚Äî remove tags from a note
- `rename-tag` ‚Äî rename a tag across all notes in a vault

## Usage

```bash
npx obsidian-mcp-rs /path/to/vault
```

MCP config (Claude Desktop / Cursor / Claude Code):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "obsidian": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "obsidian-mcp-rs", "/path/to/vault"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Tech stack

- Language: Rust (stable)
- MCP library: rmcp 2.2 (stdio transport)
- CLI: clap
- Distributed via npm optional dependencies pattern

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/obsidian-mcp-rs
- GitHub: https://github.com/MrRefactoring/obsidian-mcp-rs
- Issues: https://github.com/MrRefactoring/obsidian-mcp-rs/issues
